_all_text: Silver silver Ag Naturally alloys with gold (electrum) and mercury (amalgam). Highest electrical and thermal conductivity of any element. Fresh specimens show brilliant silver-white metallic luster. Tarnish develops complex gray-black patinas. Kongsberg wire-silver specimens are among the most celebrated native metal mineral specimens in existence. Silver has been mined since at least 3000 BCE in Anatolia. The Laurion mines near Athens funded Athenian naval power. Kongsberg, Norway produced extraordinary arborescent silver specimens prized by collectors since the 17th century. In mythology, silver is the metal of the moon — opposite gold's solar nature. Silver bullets are a recurring folkloric remedy against supernatural evil. lunar energy clarity reflection purity mystery second place (silver medal) coinage and currency (ancient Lydian, Greek, and Roman coins through modern) jewelry and silverware photography (silver halide emulsions, pre-digital era) antibacterial applications (colloidal silver, wound dressings) electrical contacts and conductors mirrors (silver backing) industrial catalysts Ag native silver wire silver arborescent silver sterling silver silver tarnish kongsberg silver native-element metal precious gold-group hydrothermal secondary jewelry currency isometric lunar-mirror cold-noble-metal mercury-adjacent aristocratic-cool silver-white gray tarnished gray-black
